name: CONTRACT-BACKLOG-19
title: Housekeeping wave: per-feature gap/dead-code/mismatch cleanup + numbered-list enforcement
description:
Comprehensive housekeeping pass across all features. Three tracks:

TRACK 1 — Numbered list enforcement (original scope):
Add contract enforcement script check-numbered-lists.py that rejects .md files using decimal sub-numbers (1.1, 1.2.3) or letter suffixes (1a, 1b) in ordered lists. Plain 1. 2. 3. only. Wire into contract test suite and Stop drift check.

TRACK 2 — Per-feature audit (spec, functional code, tests):
For each feature (contract, rabbit-cage, rabbit-feature-scope, rabbit-spec, rabbit-file, tdd-subagent, policy):
- Spec gaps: invariants documented but with no test coverage; invariants referencing deleted/renamed artifacts
- Dead code: functions, scripts, or test files no longer called or referenced
- Mismatches: spec says X, implementation does Y (silent drift)
- Test gaps: behaviors with no test; tests that pass vacuously (e.g., checking for .sh files in a Python-only repo)

TRACK 3 — Per-feature code simplification deliverable:
For each feature, produce a simplification report covering:
- Redundant abstractions to collapse
- Duplicated logic to consolidate
- Over-parameterized functions to inline
- Defensive checks for impossible scenarios to remove
- Use the `simplify` skill on changed code
File each simplification opportunity as a backlog item; work in priority order.

Suggested approach: one subagent per feature in parallel, each reads spec + implementation + tests and emits a structured audit report. Main session aggregates and files bugs/backlogs for each finding, then works them in priority order.

WAVE PROTOCOL — Out-of-scope findings (intra- and cross-feature):
During each audit wave, every finding that falls outside the current wave's stated goal — whether it is a cross-feature dependency OR an in-scope-feature finding that does not match this wave's specific objective (e.g., a bug discovered while simplifying, a spec gap noticed while auditing tests, an unrelated dead-code patch spotted during a mismatch sweep) — gets filed immediately as a bug or backlog item and deferred to a future wave. Do not fix anything inline that was not the wave's stated goal. Each wave completes its own narrow scope cleanly; the next wave picks up the filed items. Repeat until no new items are filed during a wave — that is the definition of clean. This prevents scope creep within a wave while ensuring nothing is silently dropped.

history.note: Superseded by broader housekeeping backlog item filed at higher priority.
Reopening to update in place — supersession approach was wrong, update subcommand exists.
Escalated: expanded into full housekeeping wave covering per-feature gap/dead-code/mismatch/simplification audit across spec, functional code, and tests.

history.old_value: Enforcement: numbered headings/lists in .md files must use plain 1/2/3 (no decimal sub-numbers or letter suffixes)
history.new_value: Housekeeping wave: per-feature gap/dead-code/mismatch cleanup + numbered-list enforcement
history.note: Expanded scope beyond original numbered-list lint check.

history.old_value:
## Source

User audit 2026-05-19.

## Goal

Enforce a single numbering style across rabbit-workflow .md files: plain integers — 1, 2, 3, 4 — for any numbered index (heading or list).

Forbid:
- Decimal sub-numbers: `2.6`, `3.1.4`
- Letter suffixes:     `3a`, `4b`, `5.a`
- Roman numerals:      `i`, `ii`, `iii`
- Mixed cases:         `2.a`, `1b`

## Scope (in)

- `.claude/features/**/docs/spec/*.md`
- `.claude/features/**/skills/**/SKILL.md`
- `.claude/features/**/agents/**/*.md`
- `.claude/features/policy/*.md`
- `.claude/features/contract/docs/**/*.md`
- Top-level `CLAUDE.md`, `README.md`

## Scope (out)

- `archive/**` — frozen artifacts
- `docs/superpowers/**` — brainstorm specs may quote external references with their own numbering
- `bug-backlog-files` branch content — items can quote anything from upstream issues

## Patterns to flag

At line start (after optional `##` / `###` / `####` / `-` / `*` / `+` or whitespace):

- `^\s*#{1,6}\s+\d+\.\d+`         # `## 2.6 Foo`
- `^\s*#{1,6}\s+\d+[a-z]\b`       # `## 3a Foo`
- `^\s*[-*+]?\s*\d+\.\d+\.\s`     # `1.2. ...` list item
- `^\s*[-*+]?\s*\d+[a-z][.):]`    # `3a) ...` list item

(Patterns to be adjusted during implementation as edge cases surface.)

## Implementation note

**Direct edit, no TDD.** This is a one-shot validator + cleanup:

1. Add `.claude/features/contract/scripts/enforcement/check-md-numbering.py`
2. Run it against the in-scope set; fix any current violations by rewriting to plain 1/2/3
3. Commit script + fixes in one PR
4. Hook into existing enforcement runner if there is a central runner; otherwise leave it as a runnable script (callers add it later)

The check is pure regex over .md files — no need for a spec → test → impl cycle. The one-time scope override is appropriate.

## Affected files (estimated)

- 1 new script: `scripts/enforcement/check-md-numbering.py`
- Likely 0–10 .md files needing renumbering (no concrete count yet)

## Out of scope

- Style guide for OTHER markdown conventions (heading depth, code-block language tags, list bullet style, etc.) — separate concerns if needed
- Auto-fix mode — the check just flags; humans rewrite

## Open at implementation

- Should the check be wired into a Stop-hook or just runnable on demand? (Probably on-demand for now; the violation rate is low and the rule is style-only.)
- Should the brainstorm spec docs at `docs/superpowers/specs/` be in or out of scope? (Proposed: out — those quote external numbering during research and may legitimately contain `2.6` etc.)

Comprehensive housekeeping pass across all features. Two tracks:

TRACK 1 — Numbered list enforcement (original scope):
Add contract enforcement script check-numbered-lists.py that rejects .md files using decimal sub-numbers (1.1, 1.2.3) or letter suffixes (1a, 1b) in ordered lists. Plain 1. 2. 3. only. Wire into contract test suite and Stop drift check.

TRACK 2 — Per-feature audit (spec, functional code, tests):
For each feature (contract, rabbit-cage, rabbit-feature-scope, rabbit-spec, rabbit-file, tdd-subagent, policy):
- Spec gaps: invariants documented but with no test coverage; invariants referencing deleted/renamed artifacts
- Dead code: functions, scripts, or test files no longer called or referenced

Suggested approach: one subagent per feature in parallel, each reads spec + implementation + tests and emits a structured audit report. Main session aggregates and files bugs/backlogs for each finding, then works them in priority order.

WAVE PROTOCOL — Cross-feature dependency handling:
During each audit wave, if a finding in one feature reveals a dependency or required change in another feature, file it immediately as a bug or backlog item (do not fix it inline). Each wave completes its own feature scope cleanly, then the next wave picks up the filed cross-feature items. Repeat until no new cross-feature items are filed — that is the definition of clean. This prevents scope creep within a wave while ensuring nothing is silently dropped.
history.note: Added wave protocol: cross-feature dependencies found during audit must be filed as bugs/backlogs and handled wave-by-wave until clean.

history.note: Broadened wave protocol: any out-of-scope finding (including intra-feature items not matching the current wave's goal) gets filed for the next wave, not just cross-feature dependencies.

Wave complete: 14/15 items worked (1 failed: RABBIT-FILE-BUG-34 — subagent did not complete TDD cycle on 2 attempts; left open). Track 1 (numbered-list lint) delivered. See final report from Claude session.
